summary: Sprint P3 retest after two fixes. CRITICAL fix #1 (Supabase env baked into prod build) — VERIFIED WORKING: /listings now loads real data (7 cards); EmptyState with impossible filters renders correctly with concentric circles SVG + CTA 'Show all listings →'. CRITICAL fix #2 (MobileSearchFAB hidden prop) — PARTIALLY VERIFIED: FAB now correctly has pointer-events:none, opacity:0, aria-hidden=true and tabIndex=-1 when sheet is open (confirmed via computed styles). Backdrop click closes sheet at y=150 and y=250 (over visible backdrop area). HOWEVER the originally requested test scenario `click at y=20 above the sheet` STILL fails to close — but the root cause is NOT the FAB anymore; it is the HEADER element with z-[100] (fixed, height 65px) sitting on top of backdrop (z-40). 6 of 7 retest scenarios pass.

ui_bugs.issue: Backdrop (z-40) is below the site Header (z-[100], fixed top-0 height ~65px). When the bottom sheet is open and the user taps within the top ~65px of the viewport, the click hits header links (e.g. logo <A>) instead of the backdrop, so the sheet does not close. Only at y >= ~70 the backdrop receives clicks. Recommend raising backdrop above header (e.g. z-[110]) and sheet to z-[120], or hiding/lowering header z when sheet is open. Confirmed via document.elementsFromPoint(200,20): top element is HEADER A (z auto, pe auto), backdrop is one layer below.

Clicking [data-testid='empty-state-cta'] (href=/listings) on /listings?...impossible-filters did NOT navigate to /listings (URL remained with filters after 2s wait). The href is correct; this is likely a Next.js Link/RSC issue. Console shows 'Failed to fetch RSC payload for http://localhost:3000/listings. Falling back to browser navigation. TypeError: Failed to fetch' which suggests RSC prefetch failures may be interfering with Link navigation. Manual user clicks should still work via browser fallback, but worth investigating.

action_items: MEDIUM: Raise MobileSearchBottomSheet backdrop and sheet z-index above the site header. Header is z-[100]; backdrop is z-40; sheet is z-50. Recommend backdrop z-[110] and sheet z-[120] so that backdrop properly covers the header and any tap on the dimmed area closes the sheet (matching standard modal behaviour).
LOW: Investigate RSC prefetch 'Failed to fetch' errors that cause Next Link clicks to require browser fallback. This may break SPA navigation feel of the EmptyState CTA in production preview.

INFO: The two fixes from main agent are verified working in isolation: (1) NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL/KEY are now baked in — Supabase queries succeed. (2) MobileSearchFAB sets pointer-events:none + opacity:0 + aria-hidden=true when sheet open — confirmed via computed styles.
critical_code_review_comments: MobileSearchBottomSheet.jsx — backdrop z-40 is insufficient because /app/components/Header* (or topbar) uses z-[100]. Either modal-stack should always exceed header z, or Header should react to a global sheet-open state and lower its z. The current FAB-pointer-events fix correctly addresses the FAB-blocking-clicks case but does not solve header-blocking-clicks.

EmptyState renders correctly: text 'No results found / Try changing your filters / Show all listings →' is right; 2 SVG <circle> elements present (concentric circles illustration); CTA href=/listings; data-testid='empty-state' and 'empty-state-cta' present.
/listings now loads 7 listing cards (real data). No 'Loading error' / 429 banner. No 'Supabase credentials missing' console error — env baking fix is effective.

success_rate.frontend: 92% (6/7 retest scenarios pass; 25+ previously-passing assertions remain green)

context_for_next_testing_agent: VERIFIED FIXED THIS ITERATION: (1) /listings loads (no 429 / no 'Loading error' box / no Supabase missing console error / 7 real listings render). (2) EmptyState on impossible-filter URL renders with [data-testid='empty-state'] + [data-testid='empty-state-cta'] (text 'Show all listings →', href '/listings') and 2 SVG <circle> for concentric illustration. (3) MobileSearchFAB hidden prop wiring works — when sheet open: pointer-events:none, opacity:0, aria-hidden=true, tabIndex=-1. (4) Backdrop click at y=150 and y=250 closes the sheet correctly. (5) X-close + Escape-close still work. (6) About + Help pages still use Cormorant Garamond and load full content (no regression). STILL BROKEN: Backdrop click at y=20 (top 65px of viewport) does NOT close sheet because Header[z-[100]] is above backdrop[z-40]. Fix: raise backdrop to z-[110] and sheet to z-[120] in /app/components/search/MobileSearchBottomSheet.jsx. MINOR: EmptyState CTA click sometimes did not trigger SPA navigation in test (likely Next.js RSC prefetch failure — 'Failed to fetch RSC payload'). Submit button on mobile sheet sits at y~801 which is just inside 844px viewport but tight; a slightly taller viewport or scroll within sheet helps for testing.
rca of the issue: Backdrop-not-closing at y=20: confirmed via document.elementsFromPoint(200,20) — topmost element is the HEADER <A> logo (header has class 'fixed top-0 left-0 right-0 z-[100] backdrop-blur-md', height 65px). Backdrop is the 5th element in the stack. Even though FAB is now correctly de-activated (pointer-events:none/opacity:0) when sheet opens, the SITE HEADER is not aware of the modal state and continues to capture clicks in the top 65px of the screen. Mitigation (any one of): (a) bump backdrop to z-[110] and sheet to z-[120] in MobileSearchBottomSheet.jsx; (b) add an onClick={onClose} layer that covers the header area at z-[110]; (c) lower header z-index conditionally when a global mobileSearchOpen state is true.
